rationale: Major friction: this is a live-action UGC reaction clip referencing a word/puzzle game ("Crossword Go"), with a muted blue/beige palette and zero gameplay — none of it conveys Mob Control's signature mob-growth, multiplier gates, or vibrant purple/cyan/yellow stylized 3D look that drives IPM in this genre. The dancing-satisfaction hook also skews older/casual-puzzle audience rather than the 18-35 male hyper-casual action player. Only run as a low-budget test if the team wants to explore UGC framing, and only if reshot with on-screen Mob Control gameplay B-roll and a hook tied to "satisfying crowd-multiplier" moments — otherwise skip.
